added_content.ex_information:
Complete the notes below.
Write
ONE WORD ONLY
for each answer.

sentence.content: For my presentation today, I’m going to talk about the Céide Fields in the northwest of Ireland, one of the largest Neolithic sites in the world.
sentence.content_zh: 在今天的演讲中，我将介绍位于爱尔兰西北部的塞尔德田野，这是世界上最大的新石器时代遗址之一。

sentence.content: I recently visited this site and observed the work that is currently being done by a team of archaeologists there.
sentence.content_zh: 我最近参观了这个遗址，并观察了考古队目前正在那里开展的工作。

sentence.content: The site was first discovered in the 1930s by a local teacher, Patrick Caulfield.
sentence.content_zh: 该遗址于 20 世纪 30 年代由当地教师帕特里克·考尔菲尔德首次发现。

sentence.content: He noticed that when local people were digging in the bog, they were constantly hitting against what seemed to be rows of stones.
sentence.content_zh: 他注意到，当地人在沼泽地里挖掘时，不断撞击到似乎是一排排的石头。

sentence.content: He realised that these must be walls and that they must be thousands of years old for them to predate the bog which subsequently grew over them.
sentence.content_zh: 他意识到这些一定是城墙，而且这些墙壁一定有数千年的历史，在沼泽形成之前就已经存在了。

sentence.content: He wrote to the National Museum in Dublin to ask them to investigate, but no one took him seriously.
sentence.content_zh: 他写信给都柏林国家博物馆，要求他们进行调查，但是没有人把他的话当回事。

sentence.content: It wasn’t until 40 years later, when Patrick Caulfield’s son Seamus, who had become an archaeologist by then, began to explore further.
sentence.content_zh: 直到 40 年后，帕特里克·考尔菲尔德的儿子西莫才开始进一步探索，这时的西莫已经成为了一位考古学家。

sentence.content: He inserted iron probes into the bog to map the formation of the stones, a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel buried in the bog for thousands of years.
sentence.content_zh: 他将铁探测针插入沼泽，绘制石头的形成图。千百年来，当地人一直用这种传统方法寻找埋在沼泽中的燃料。

sentence.content: Carbon dating later proved that the site was over 5,000 years old and was the largest Neolithic site in Ireland.
sentence.content_zh: 碳年代测定法后来证明，该遗址距今已有 5000 多年的历史，是爱尔兰最大的新石器时代遗址。

sentence.content: Thanks to the bog which covers the area, the remains of the settlement at Céide Fields, which is over 5,000 years old, are extremely well-preserved.
sentence.content_zh: 由于该地区被沼泽覆盖，塞尔德田野定居点的遗迹保存完好，距今已有 5000 多年的历史。

sentence.content: A bog is 90 percent water; its soil is so saturated that when the grasses and heathers that grow on its surface die, they don’t fully decay but accumulate in layers.
sentence.content_zh: 沼泽里90% 都是水；因为沼泽中土壤水分饱和，当生长在其表面的草和石楠死亡后，它们不会完全腐烂，而是层层堆积。

sentence.content: Objects remain so well preserved in these conditions because of the acidity of the peat and the deficiency of oxygen.
sentence.content_zh: 由于泥炭的酸性和氧气的匮乏，物件在这种条件下保存得十分完好。

sentence.content: At least 175 days of rain a year are required for this to happen; this part of Ireland gets an average of 225 days.
sentence.content_zh: 要做到这一点，每年至少需要 175 天的降雨；爱尔兰这一地区的年平均降雨天数为 225 天。

sentence.content: The Neolithic farmers at Céide would have enjoyed several centuries of relative peace and stability.
sentence.content_zh: 塞尔德新石器时代的农民应该享受了几个世纪的相对和平与稳定。

sentence.content: Neolithic farmers generally lived in larger communities than their predecessors, with a number of houses built around a community building.
sentence.content_zh: 新石器时代的农民通常比他们的祖先生活在更大的社群中，社群建筑周围建有许多房屋。

sentence.content: As they lived in permanent settlements, Neolithic farmers were able to build bigger houses.
sentence.content_zh: 由于他们确定了永久定居点，新石器时代的农民可以建造更大的房屋。

sentence.content: These weren’t round as people often assume, but rectangular with a small hole in the roof that allowed smoke to escape.
sentence.content_zh: 这些房屋并不像人们通常认为的那样是圆形的，而是长方形的，屋顶上有一个小洞，可以让烟雾排出。

sentence.content: This is one of many innovations and indicates that the Neolithic farmers were the first people to cook indoors.
sentence.content_zh: 这也是新石器时代的许多创新之一。同时表明新石器时代的农民是第一批在室内做饭的人。

sentence.content: Another new technology that Neolithic settlers brought to Ireland was pottery.
sentence.content_zh: 新石器时代定居者带到爱尔兰的另一项新技术是制陶。

sentence.content: Fragments of Neolithic pots have been found in Céide and elsewhere in Ireland.
sentence.content_zh: 在塞尔德和爱尔兰其他地方发现了新石器时代的陶器碎片。

sentence.content: The pots were used for many things; as well as for storing food, pots were filled with a small amount of fat and when this was set alight, they served as lamps.
sentence.content_zh: 这些陶罐有很多用途：除了储存食物，陶罐里还装有少量油脂，点燃后可以作为灯具。

sentence.content: It’s thought that the Céide Fields were mainly used as paddocks for animals to graze in.
sentence.content_zh: 人们认为，塞尔德田野主要是供牲畜吃草的围场。

sentence.content: Evidence from the Céide Fields suggests that each plot of land was of a suitable size to sustain an extended family.
sentence.content_zh: 来自塞尔德田野的证据表明，每块土地的面积都适合维持一个大家庭的生活。

sentence.content: They may have used a system of rotational grazing in order to prevent over-grazing and to allow for plant recovery and regrowth.
sentence.content_zh: 他们可能使用了轮牧制度，以防止过度放牧，并使植物得以恢复和重新生长。

sentence.content: This must have been a year-round activity as no structures have been found which would have been used to shelter animals in the winter.
sentence.content_zh: 由于没有发现用于牲畜冬季栖息的建筑，因此这种轮牧活动肯定是全年进行的。

sentence.content: However, archaeologists believe that this way of life at Céide ceased abruptly. Why was this?
sentence.content_zh: 然而，考古学家认为，塞尔德的这种生活方式突然终止了。这是为什么呢？

sentence.content: Well, several factors may have contributed to the changing circumstances.
sentence.content_zh: 有几个因素可能导致了环境的变化。

sentence.content: The soil would have become less productive and led to the abandonment of farming.
sentence.content_zh: 土壤的生产力降低，导致人们放弃耕作。

sentence.content: The crop rotationsystem was partly responsible for this as it would have been very intensive and was not sustainable.
sentence.content_zh: 轮作制度是造成这种情况的部分原因，因为这种制度的生产周期非常密集，无法可持续。

sentence.content: But there were also climatic pressures too. The farmers at Céide would have enjoyed a relatively dry period, but this began to change and the conditions became wetter as there was a lot more rain.
sentence.content_zh: 但气候压力也是原因之一。塞尔德的农民曾经度过了一段相对干燥的时期，但由于雨水增多，这种情况开始发生变化，环境变得更加潮湿。

sentence.content: It was these conditions that encouraged the bog to form over the area which survives today.
sentence.content_zh: 正是这些条件促进了沼泽的形成，从而保护了沼泽下的遗迹。

sentence.content: So now I’d like to show you some...
sentence.content_zh: 现在我想展示下...

officialExplain.explain: 根据题干句意，需要填入stones曾经的情况。所填内容为表语成分，预判词性为形容词或者名词。题干中的1930s、local teacher以及stones均原词重现，且录音中都出现了重读，易于定位。stones一词后根据He realised these must be walls可得出答案。随后说到“thousands of years old…predate the bog…”，对应题干的once，可进一步验证答案。此处请参考“文本解析”第一句的翻译。
根据题干，此题需填入人的身份，且答案很可能先于题干关键词出现。题干中的became an archaeologist原词重现，前文出现答案son，答案词在录音中也有明显的重读和停顿。

题干句子稍长，需要识别其中的语法结构，“used by local people to dig for ______”作句子主语traditional method的后置定语。即“一种当地人用来挖______的传统方法被用于探测石头的位置”。题干中的traditional method原词重现，紧接着原文中也以定语从句的形式替换了题干的后置定语，不难识别，dig for替换为find，可得出答案fuel。此处请参考“文本解析”第二句的翻译。
录音原文基本还原了题干的因果逻辑结构，items替换为objects，well preserved原词重现，because of后出现lack的同义替换deficiency，随后出现答案oxygen，考生需要对deficiency的意思和发音都比较敏感，同时要熟悉oxygen的拼写。
根据题干中的in shape可知需填入表示形状的单词。houses原词重现，首先出现these weren’t round，提示在描述房屋形状，转折词but后出现答案rectangular，考生需熟悉该单词的拼写。
根据题干可知填入pots和storage并列的用途。题干词storage替换为动词store，并列词and在原文中替换为as well as，但需注意等待和make搭配合理的宾语，听到fat不可盲目填入，答案应为served as后的宾语lamps。此处请参考“文本解析”第五句的翻译。
根据题干中的冠词a预判填入名词单数。原文句型结构和题干基本一致，但替换的表达较多。each field替换为each plot of land；large enough替换为suitable size；support替换为sustain；big替换为extended；随后出现答案family。考生需要熟悉extended family这一搭配。
根据题干中的during可推断答案为某一时期。注意house此处为动词“为……提供住所”。题干中的no和structures原词重现，house替换为shelter，随后出现答案winter。
根据题干粗体字小标题可知需听取农业衰落的原因。此题为其中的一个原因，即“_____的质量下降”。decline in quality替换为less productive，不难得出答案soil。
本题和39题为列点式并列结构，而原文也在并列关联词also后引出另一个原因climatic pressures，提示此题即将出现，后文的a lot more替换题干的increase，随后出现答案rain。
